key_insight: Explicitly sampling non-ideal configurational entropy, together with vibrational entropy, significantly changes predicted binary alloy phase boundaries and lowers the AuCu order-disorder transition temperature toward experiment.
engineering_relevance: The method enables more reliable atomistic phase-diagram construction for alloy design by directly computing composition-temperature stability from free energies rather than relying on ideal-solution assumptions.
method: Extend non-equilibrium thermodynamic integration to composition-dependent transformations via alchemical interpolation of interactions.
Combine Monte Carlo identity-exchange moves with molecular dynamics to sample configurational and vibrational entropy along the integration path.
Use ACE interatomic potentials trained on DFT data (LDA, PBE, r2SCAN) to compute Au-Cu phase diagrams.
evidence_signal: For AuCu, explicit configurational sampling reduced the predicted order-disorder transition from about 810 K to 710 K (closer to the experimental 683 K) and broadened the solid-solution stability range.
watch_item: Predicted accuracy is highly sensitive to the chosen exchange-correlation functional, so agreement for one model should not be taken as broadly predictive without careful functional validation.
paper_title: Computing binary alloy phase diagrams with explicit configurational and vibrational entropy
